edge_hypothesis: MACD tail harvesters at 7:1 R:R achieved PF=1.71 on both 4h and 1h ETH (all-regime ACCEPT). The regime breakdown reveals ranging PF=2.06 — the strongest single-regime PF observed across any template. MACD histogram zero-cross inherently captures momentum INFLECTION points, not trend continuation. At wider R:R (10:1), we only need ~12% win rate for profitability. The hypothesis: by pushing R:R to 10:1 on 1h timeframe, we filter to ONLY the strongest momentum shifts — the ones where histogram acceleration sustains long enough to hit a 10x ATR target. These are the fat-tail events where momentum genuinely shifts direction (not noise). The 1h timeframe captures more granular momentum shifts than 4h, generating more trade opportunities. Combined with a 1.5x ATR stop (proven minimum safe floor), the extreme asymmetry means even a 15% win rate produces PF>1.5. Regime assumption: ALL REGIMES (MACD is the only template showing PF>1.0 in all three regimes at tail-harvester R:R). Invalidation: if win rate drops below 10% at 10:1, the momentum inflection signals don't generate enough sustained moves to justify extreme R:R — cap R:R at 8:1 for this template.

variants.name: macd_extreme_tail_1h_10to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with extreme 10:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 15.0x ATR TP). Captures only the strongest momentum inflection events. At 10:1, a 12% win rate produces PF=1.3. The 1h timeframe generates ~2x more MACD crossovers than 4h, compensating for the lower win rate at extreme TP. This variant tests whether MACD momentum shifts on 1h sustain long enough for 15x ATR moves.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive — momentum inflection).
Close > EMA_50 confirms medium-term uptrend context.
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative — momentum inflection).
Close < EMA_50 confirms medium-term downtrend context.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification (trending>25, ranging<20, transitional 20-25)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (15.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if win rate < 10%, MACD momentum inflections don't sustain for 15x ATR moves on 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 15.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend context — close vs EMA_50 for directional bias
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — momentum inflection signal, proven PF=1.71 at 7:1
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing — 1.5x stop / 15.0x TP for extreme 10:1 R:R

variants.name: macd_ultra_tail_1h_12to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with ultra-extreme 12:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 18.0x ATR TP). The maximum asymmetry variant — pushes R:R to the boundary of feasibility. At 12:1, only a 10% win rate is needed for PF=1.2. This tests the absolute upper bound of the tail harvester architecture: are there momentum moves on 1h ETH that sustain for 18x ATR? If yes, this variant captures them with minimal capital risk per trade.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive).
Close > EMA_50 confirms medium-term uptrend context.
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative).
Close < EMA_50 confirms medium-term downtrend context.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if trades < 50 or win rate < 8%, 18x ATR moves are too rare on 1h to be tradeable
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend context — directional bias filter
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — testing extreme 12:1 R:R upper bound
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing — 1.5x stop / 18.0x TP for 12:1 R:R

variants.name: macd_moderate_tail_4h_9to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 9:1 R:R (2.0x ATR stop, 18.0x ATR TP). The 4h bridge variant — tests the gap between proven 7:1 (PF=1.71) and untested 10:1. A wider 2.0x ATR stop on 4h gives more breathing room for the larger timeframe's noise, while 9:1 R:R is a previously untested point in the parameter space. If 7:1 works at PF=1.71, does 9:1 maintain PF>1.4? This answers whether the R:R-PF relationship is linear or has a cliff.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive).
Close > EMA_50 confirms medium-term uptrend context.
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative).
Close < EMA_50 confirms medium-term downtrend context.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if PF drops below 1.2 compared to 7:1's PF=1.71, R:R-PF has a cliff between 7:1 and 9:1
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend context — proven baseline from 7:1 ACCEPT
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — testing 9:1 gap between proven 7:1 and untested 10:1
ATR_14 for stop/TP — 2.0x stop (wider for 4h noise) / 18.0x TP for 9:1 R:R
